person_relationships.description: Ngien Hoon Ping succeeded Neo Kian Hong as SMRT CEO in August 2022. Both are former SAF generals.
Both are former SAF generals who served as SMRT CEO. Kuek preceded Neo Kian Hong.

major_achievements.achievement: Awarded the Public Administration Medal (Silver) for civil service.
Appointed Chief Executive of LTA, overseeing Singapore's land transport policy and infrastructure.
Appointed Group CEO of SMRT Corporation, Singapore's largest rail and bus operator.

biography_summary: Ngien Hoon Ping is a Singaporean public service and transport executive who has served as Group Chief Executive Officer and Executive Director of SMRT Corporation Ltd since August 2022. He is the third consecutive former SAF general to lead SMRT. Prior to SMRT, he was CEO of FairPrice Group's Supply Chain Business (2020-2022), Chief Executive of the Land Transport Authority (2016-2020), and Deputy Secretary (Performance) at the Ministry of Finance. A former Brigadier General in the Singapore Armed Forces, he served over 15 years in maintenance, engineering, logistics and operations. He holds a Bachelor of Engineering from the University of Manchester and a Master of Science from NUS. He is a Fellow of the Institute of Engineers and has attended executive programmes at INSEAD and IMD.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with data from SMRT official website, Straits Times, and Yahoo News. Career history spanning SAF, MOF, LTA, FairPrice, and SMRT added. Education, awards, and relationships detailed.
